refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level. Refining it into sub-FRs focused on monitoring energy use, controlling operations to reduce energy, and optimizing system performance aligns with the energy efficiency KPI and supports verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and comparing to E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy; confirm control actions reduce energy use; demonstrate optimization algorithms improve efficiency.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage.
FR3: Optimize system parameters to achieve energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy sensors and data acquisition system for monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive control algorithms for vehicle operation.
DP3: Use predictive analytics and feedback loops for optimization.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address the refined FRs comprehensively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring and control modules.
Feedback loops between optimization and control.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and managed via well-defined data interfaces; modular design reduces risk.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable sensor data and communication links.
Risk of control algorithm complexity impacting real-time performance.
Optimization effectiveness depends on quality of predictive models.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing optimization separately to isolate complexity and facilitate updates.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system-level integration of monitoring, control, and optimization functions for energy efficiency.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for transmitting sensor data and receiving control commands.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational tasks and parameters from higher-level management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between modules and external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as it integrates all energy efficiency functions; low diffusion risk due to centralized architecture.
Contains physical sensors and actuators but limited control logic; moderate diffusion risk if control logic is duplicated.
Primarily for remote control; energy efficiency functions are peripheral, higher diffusion risk if energy control logic is duplicated here.
Focus on safety alerts; minimal role in energy efficiency; high diffusion risk if energy functions are mixed here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing energy efficiency functions within the NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential performance bottlenecks if all functions are centralized.
Integration complexity may increase testing effort.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting optimization into a submodule within NGHE Solution to balance containment and performance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FRs are well-defined and mapped to logical DPs that address monitoring, control, and optimization comprehensively.

diffusion_risks: Risk of control logic duplication if not properly centralized.
Potential interface complexity between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Centralizing in NGHE Solution module reduces diffusion; interfaces are well-defined and use known communication links.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and operational testing within the NGHE Solution module boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of functions to NGHE Solution with defined interfaces to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

recommended_revisions: Consider modularizing optimization function within NGHE Solution for scalability.
Clarify interface protocols between NGHE Solution and Vehicle for control commands.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meets the requirements and passes gate reviews with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage.
FR3: Optimize system parameters to achieve energy efficiency target.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; recommended improvements are for future refinement.

assumptions: Reliable sensor data and communication infrastructure.
Modular architecture supports integration and scalability.
open_risks: Performance impact of centralized control and optimization.
Complexity of interface management between modules.
evidence_needed: Verification data of energy_per_ton metric over evaluation period.
